text.title: enum-constraints-closed-vocabularies Output Contract
text.summary: Pydantic and JSON Schema forms for closed enums and the semi-open union escape hatch.

section.title: Closed enum (Pydantic)
section.code:
from typing import Literal from pydantic import BaseModel
class Ticket(BaseModel): category: Literal["billing", "tech_support", "refund", "spam", "other"] priority: Literal["P0", "P1", "P2", "P3"] sentiment: Literal["angry", "frustrated", "neutral", "happy"]

section.title: Semi-open via discriminated union

example.note: Every value is from the closed enum; strict-mode decoder cannot emit any other token sequence.
Capitalisation drift, free-string priority, novel sentiment label. Validator rejects all three.

list.item: Every classification field uses `Literal[...]` (Pydantic) or `enum` (JSON Schema).
Strict mode or a constrained decoder is configured at the provider call site.
Semi-open sets use a discriminated union with explicit "novel" branch.
Retry-with-feedback or mapping table exists for JSON-mode fallback paths.
